title: High Dimensional Example of MultiEFM
text: First, generate simulated data heavy tail, error term follows multivariate t-distribution degree freedom 3. simulate high-dimensional scenario, significantly increase feature dimension p = 500.
code: set.seed(1) nu <- 3 # nu is set to p <- 500 # High-dimensional setting nvec <- c(150,200);  q <- 3; qs <- c(2,2); S <- length(nvec) sigma2_eps <- 1 datList <- gendata_simu_robust(seed=1, nvec=nvec, p=p, q=q, qs=qs, rho=c(5,5), err.type='mvt', nu=nu) XList <- datList$Xlist

title: High Dimensional Example of MultiEFM
text: Fit MultiEFM model using function MultiEFM() R package MultiEFM. Users can use ?MultiEFM see details function. two matrices D̂\widehat D DD, use trace statistic measure similarity. trace statistic ranges 0 1, higher values indicating better performance.
code: methodNames <- c("MultiEFM", "MSFA-CAVI", "MSFA-SVI") metricMat <- matrix(NA, nrow=length(methodNames), ncol=5) colnames(metricMat) <- c('A_tr', 'B_tr',  'F_tr', 'H_tr', 'Time') row.names(metricMat) <- methodNames  tic <- proc.time() res <- MultiEFM(XList, q=q, qs_vec=qs) toc <- proc.time()  metricMat["MultiEFM",'Time'] <- toc[3] - tic[3] metricMat["MultiEFM",'A_tr'] <- trace_statistic_fun(res$A, datList$A0) metricMat["MultiEFM",'B_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(res$B, datList$Blist0) metricMat["MultiEFM",'F_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(res$F, datList$Flist) metricMat["MultiEFM",'H_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(res$H, datList$Hlist)

title: High Dimensional Example of MultiEFM
text: compare MultiEFM two prominent methods: MSFA-CAVI MSFA-SVI First, implement MSFA-CAVI: Next, implement MSFA-SVI:
code: X_s <- lapply(XList, scale, scale=FALSE) ### MSFA-CAVI print("MSFA-CAVI") tic <- proc.time() cavi_est <- cavi_msfa(X_s,  K=q, J_s=qs) toc <- proc.time() time_cavi <- toc[3] - tic[3]  hF_cavi <- hH_cavi <- list() for(s in 1:S){   hF_cavi[[s]] <- t(Reduce(cbind, cavi_est$mean_f[[s]]))   hH_cavi[[s]] <- t(Reduce(cbind, cavi_est$mean_l[[s]])) }  metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'Time']  <- time_cavi metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'A_tr']  <- trace_statistic_fun(cavi_est$mean_phi, datList$A0) metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'B_tr']  <- trace_list_fun(cavi_est$mean_lambda_s, datList$Blist0) metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'F_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(hF_cavi, datList$Flist) metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'H_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(hH_cavi, datList$Hlist) print("MSFA-SVI") tic <- proc.time() svi_est <- svi_msfa(X_s, K=q, J_s=qs, verbose = 0) toc <- proc.time() time_svi <- toc[3] - tic[3]  hF_svi <- hH_svi <- list() for(s in 1:S){   hF_svi[[s]] <- t(Reduce(cbind, svi_est$mean_f[[s]]))   hH_svi[[s]] <- t(Reduce(cbind, svi_est$mean_l[[s]])) }  metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'Time']  <- time_svi metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'A_tr']  <- trace_statistic_fun(svi_est$mean_phi, datList$A0) metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'B_tr']  <- trace_list_fun(svi_est$mean_lambda_s, datList$Blist0) metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'F_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(hF_svi, datList$Flist) metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'H_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(hH_svi, datList$Hlist)

title: High Dimensional Example of MultiEFM
text: Next, summarized metrics MultiEFM compared methods data.frame object. Plot results MultiEFM methods, suggests MultiEFM achieves better estimation accuracy study-shared loading matrix , study-specified loading matrix B factor matrix H. MultiEFM significantly outperforms compared methods terms estimation accuracy B H, well computational efficiency.
code: dat_metric <- data.frame(metricMat) dat_metric$Method <- factor(row.names(dat_metric), levels=row.names(dat_metric)) my_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 14) +    theme(legend.position = "none",           plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5),          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))   p1 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(A_tr)), aes(x= Method, y=A_tr, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Shared Loading (A)", x=NULL, y="Trace") + my_theme p2 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(F_tr)), aes(x= Method, y=F_tr, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Shared Factor (F)", x=NULL, y="Trace") + my_theme p3 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(B_tr)), aes(x= Method, y=B_tr, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Specific Loading (B)", x=NULL, y="Trace") + my_theme p4 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(H_tr)), aes(x= Method, y=H_tr, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Specific Factor (H)", x=NULL, y="Trace") + my_theme p5 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(Time)), aes(x= Method, y=Time, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Time (s)", x=NULL, y="Seconds") + my_theme  plot_grid(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, nrow=2, ncol=3)

title: High Dimensional Example of MultiEFM
text: applied proposed TSP method select number factors. results showed method potential identify true values.
code: hq_res <- selectFac.MultiEFM(XList, q_max=10, qs_max=5, verbose = FALSE) message("Estimated shared q = ", hq_res$hq, " VS true q = ", q) sessionInfo()

title: Low Dimensional Example of MultiEFM
text: First, generate simulated data heavy tail, error term follows multivariate t-distribution degree freedom 3.
code: set.seed(1) nu <- 3 # nu is set to p <- 100 nvec <- c(150,200);  q <- 3; qs <- c(2,2); S <- length(nvec) sigma2_eps <- 1 datList <- gendata_simu_robust(seed=1, nvec=nvec, p=p, q=q, qs=qs, rho=c(5,5), err.type='mvt', nu=nu) XList <- datList$Xlist

title: Low Dimensional Example of MultiEFM
text: Fit MultiEFM model using function ‘MultiEFM()’ R package ‘MultiEFM’. Users can use ‘?MultiEFM’ see details function. two matrices D̂\widehat D DD, use trace statistic measure similarity. trace statistic ranges 0 1, higher values indicating better performance.
code: methodNames <- c("MultiEFM", "MSFA-CAVI", "MSFA-SVI") metricMat <- matrix(NA, nrow=length(methodNames), ncol=5) colnames(metricMat) <- c('A_tr', 'B_tr',  'F_tr', 'H_tr', 'Time') row.names(metricMat) <- methodNames  tic <- proc.time() res <- MultiEFM(XList, q=q, qs_vec=qs) #> Iter 1: Obj = 9854.132605, rel change = 9.954e+01 #> Iter 2: Obj = 9854.132605, rel change = 1.846e-16 toc <- proc.time()  metricMat["MultiEFM",'Time'] <- toc[3] - tic[3] metricMat["MultiEFM",'A_tr'] <- trace_statistic_fun(res$A, datList$A0) metricMat["MultiEFM",'B_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(res$B, datList$Blist0) metricMat["MultiEFM",'F_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(res$F, datList$Flist) metricMat["MultiEFM",'H_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(res$H, datList$Hlist)

title: Low Dimensional Example of MultiEFM
text: compare MultiEFM two prominent methods: MSFA-CAVI MSFA-SVI First, implement MSFA-CAVI:
code: X_s <- lapply(XList, scale, scale=FALSE) ### MSFA-CAVI print("MSFA-CAVI") #> [1] "MSFA-CAVI" tic <- proc.time() cavi_est <- cavi_msfa(X_s,  K=q, J_s=qs) #> [1] "Iteration:    1, Objective: 2.78559e+03, Relative improvement Inf" #> [1] "Iteration:    1, Objective: 1.69256e+02, Relative improvement Inf" #> [1] "Iteration:    1, Objective: 3.49808e+02, Relative improvement Inf" #> [1] "Algorithm converged in 5 iterations." toc <- proc.time() time_cavi <- toc[3] - tic[3]  hF_cavi <- hH_cavi <- list() for(s in 1:S){   hF_cavi[[s]] <- t(Reduce(cbind, cavi_est$mean_f[[s]]))   hH_cavi[[s]] <- t(Reduce(cbind, cavi_est$mean_l[[s]])) }  metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'Time']  <- time_cavi metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'A_tr']  <- trace_statistic_fun(cavi_est$mean_phi, datList$A0) metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'B_tr']  <- trace_list_fun(cavi_est$mean_lambda_s, datList$Blist0) metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'F_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(hF_cavi, datList$Flist) metricMat["MSFA-CAVI",'H_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(hH_cavi, datList$Hlist) print("MSFA-SVI") #> [1] "MSFA-SVI" tic <- proc.time() svi_est <- svi_msfa(X_s, K=q, J_s=qs, verbose = 0) #> [1] "Iteration:    1, Objective: 2.78929e+03, Relative improvement Inf" #> [1] "Iteration:    1, Objective: 1.67785e+02, Relative improvement Inf" #> [1] "Iteration:    1, Objective: 3.50766e+02, Relative improvement Inf" #> [1] "iteration 1 finished" #> [1] "iteration 2 finished" #> [1] "iteration 3 finished" #> [1] "iteration 4 finished" #> [1] "iteration 5 finished" toc <- proc.time() time_svi <- toc[3] - tic[3]  hF_svi <- hH_svi <- list() for(s in 1:S){   hF_svi[[s]] <- t(Reduce(cbind, svi_est$mean_f[[s]]))   hH_svi[[s]] <- t(Reduce(cbind, svi_est$mean_l[[s]])) }  metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'Time']  <- time_svi metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'A_tr']  <- trace_statistic_fun(svi_est$mean_phi, datList$A0) metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'B_tr']  <- trace_list_fun(svi_est$mean_lambda_s, datList$Blist0) metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'F_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(hF_svi, datList$Flist) metricMat["MSFA-SVI",'H_tr'] <- trace_list_fun(hH_svi, datList$Hlist)

title: Low Dimensional Example of MultiEFM
text: Next, summarized metrics MultiEFM compared methods data.frame object. Plot results MultiEFM methods, suggests MultiEFM achieves better estimation accuracy study-shared loading matrix , study-specified loading matrix B factor matrix H. MultiEFM significantly outperforms compared methods terms estimation accuracy B H, well computational efficiency.
code: dat_metric <- data.frame(metricMat) dat_metric$Method <- factor(row.names(dat_metric), levels=row.names(dat_metric)) library(cowplot) library(ggplot2)  my_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 14) +    theme(legend.position = "none",           plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5),          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))   p1 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(A_tr)), aes(x= Method, y=A_tr, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Shared Loading (A)", x=NULL, y="Trace") + my_theme p2 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(F_tr)), aes(x= Method, y=F_tr, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Shared Factor (F)", x=NULL, y="Trace") + my_theme p3 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(B_tr)), aes(x= Method, y=B_tr, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Specific Loading (B)", x=NULL, y="Trace") + my_theme p4 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(H_tr)), aes(x= Method, y=H_tr, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Specific Factor (H)", x=NULL, y="Trace") + my_theme p5 <- ggplot(data=subset(dat_metric, !is.na(Time)), aes(x= Method, y=Time, fill=Method)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + labs(title="Time (s)", x=NULL, y="Seconds") + my_theme plot_grid(p1,p2,p3, p4,  p5, nrow=2, ncol=3)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Wei Liu. Author, maintainer. Ruihan Zhang. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Liu W, Zhang R (2026). MultiEFM: Robust Estimation Multi-Study Elliptical Factor Model. R package version 0.1.2, https://github.com/feiyoung/MultiEFM.
code: @Manual{,   title = {MultiEFM: Robust Estimation for Multi-Study Elliptical Factor Model},   author = {Wei Liu and Ruihan Zhang},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.2},   url = {https://github.com/feiyoung/MultiEFM}, }

what: Generate Simulated Data for Robust Multi-Study Elliptical Factor Model — gendata_simu_robust
title: Generate Simulated Data for Robust Multi-Study Elliptical Factor Model — gendata_simu_robust
text: Generate simulated data high-dimensional multi-study factor analysis robust elliptical distributions (Multivariate t-distribution). data follows factor model common factors (shared across studies) study-specific factors, plus noise.

title: Generate Simulated Data for Robust Multi-Study Elliptical Factor Model — gendata_simu_robust
code: gendata_simu_robust(   seed = 1,   nvec = c(100, 300),   p = 50,   q = 3,   qs = rep(2, length(nvec)),   fac.type = c("gaussian", "mvt"),   err.type = c("gaussian", "mvt"),   rho = c(1, 1),   sigma2_eps = 0.1,   nu = 1 )

title: Generate Simulated Data for Robust Multi-Study Elliptical Factor Model — gendata_simu_robust
text: seed Integer, default = 1. Random seed reproducibility. nvec Numeric vector (length >= 2). Sample sizes study. p Integer, default = 50. Number variables (features) data. q Integer, default = 3. Number common factors (shared across studies). qs Numeric vector. Number study-specific factors study. fac.type Character, default = "gaussian". Factor distribution type, one "gaussian" "mvt". err.type Character, default = "gaussian". Error distribution type, one "gaussian" "mvt". rho Numeric vector length 2, default = `c(1,1)`. Scaling factors common specific factor loadings. sigma2_eps Numeric, default = 0.1. Variance error term. nu Integer, default = 1. Degrees freedom multivariate t-distribution ("mvt").

title: Generate Simulated Data for Robust Multi-Study Elliptical Factor Model — gendata_simu_robust
text: list containing simulated data true parameter values: Xlist: List data matrices (ns × p) study. mu0: True mean vector matrix. A0: True common factor loadings matrix. Blist0: List true study-specific factor loadings matrices. Flist: List true common factor score matrices. Hlist: List true study-specific factor score matrices. q: Number common factors used. qs: Number study-specific factors used.

what: Fit the high-dimensional multi-study elliptical factor model — MultiEFM
title: Fit the high-dimensional multi-study elliptical factor model — MultiEFM
text: Fit high-dimensional multi-study elliptical factor model learns latent features accounts heterogeneity among sources.

title: Fit the high-dimensional multi-study elliptical factor model — MultiEFM
code: MultiEFM(   XList,   q,   qs_vec,   loads_method = c("Residual", "Ortho"),   fac_est_method = c("Huber", "LS"),   n_threads = 4,   sample_pairs = 0,   epsObj = 1e-06,   maxIter = 20,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 1 )

title: Fit the high-dimensional multi-study elliptical factor model — MultiEFM
text: XList length-S list, component represents data matrix specific study. q integer, specify number study-shared factors. qs_vec integer vector length S, specify number study-specified factors study. loads_method character string specifying method loading estimation. Options "Residual" "Ortho". fac_est_method character string specifying method factor estimation. Options "Huber" "LS" (Least Squares). n_threads integer, specify number threads parallel computing; default 4. sample_pairs integer, specify number sample pairs used estimation; default 0. epsObj positive value, tolerance convergence, default 1e-6. maxIter maximum iteration algorithm. default 20. verbose logical value, whether output information iteration. seed integer, specify random seed reproducibility initialization; default 1.

title: Fit the high-dimensional multi-study elliptical factor model — MultiEFM
text: return list including following components: estimated loading matrix corresponding study-shared factors; F list composed estimation study-shared factor matrix study; B list composed loading matrices corresponding study-specified factors; H list composed estimation study-specified factor matrix study.

title: Fit the high-dimensional multi-study elliptical factor model — MultiEFM
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ p <- 100 nvec <- c(150, 200) qs <- c(2, 2) datList <- gendata_simu_robust(seed=1, nvec=nvec, p=p, q=3, qs=qs, rho=c(5,5),                                err.type='mvt', sigma2_eps = 1, nu=3) XList <- datList$Xlist res <- MultiEFM(XList, q=3, qs_vec=qs) str(res) } # }

what: Select the number of factors for MultiEFM — selectFac.MultiEFM
title: Select the number of factors for MultiEFM — selectFac.MultiEFM
text: Automatically estimate number study-shared factors study-specific factors based eigenvalue ratio method.

title: Select the number of factors for MultiEFM — selectFac.MultiEFM
code: selectFac.MultiEFM(   XList,   q_max = 20,   qs_max = 20,   qd = NULL,   n_threads = 4,   sample_pairs = 0,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 1 )

title: Select the number of factors for MultiEFM — selectFac.MultiEFM
text: XList length-S list, component represents data matrix specific study. q_max integer, specify maximum number study-shared factors consider; default 20. qs_max integer, specify maximum number study-specified factors consider; default 20. qd optional integer, specify true number combined factors known advance; default NULL. n_threads integer, specify number threads parallel computing; default 4. sample_pairs integer, specify number sample pairs used estimation; default 0. verbose logical value, whether output estimation information. seed integer, specify random seed reproducibility; default 1.

title: Select the number of factors for MultiEFM — selectFac.MultiEFM
text: return list including following components: hq estimated number study-shared factors; hqs_vec integer vector representing estimated number study-specific factors study; eigvals_A eigenvalues used determining shared factor number; eig_ratio_A eigenvalue ratios used determining shared factor number; eigvals_B list eigenvalues used determining specific factor numbers; eig_ratio_B list eigenvalue ratios used determining specific factor numbers.
